summary: Iteration 57: Complete testing of Granular User Permissions feature. All 15 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend UI tests passed including permissions dialog, checkboxes, Select All/Clear buttons, and save functionality.

issues: Minor: Console warning about missing Description for DialogContent - accessibility improvement recommended but not blocking

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/admin/permissions/screens returns all 10 screen definitions with key, label, description
Backend: GET /api/admin/permissions/screens returns 401 without auth
Backend: GET /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions returns user_id and allowed_screens
Backend: GET /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions returns 404 for non-existent user
Backend: GET /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions returns 401 without auth
Backend: PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions updates allowed_screens successfully
Backend: PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions with all screens works
Backend: PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions with empty list clears restrictions (full access)
Backend: PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions filters out invalid screen keys
Backend: PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions returns 404 for non-existent user
Backend: PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions returns 401 without auth
Backend: Login response includes allowed_screens field in user object
Backend: GET /api/auth/me includes allowed_screens field
Backend: GET /api/admin/all-users returns allowed_screens for each user
Backend: Full permissions E2E workflow (get screens → set → verify → clear)
Frontend: All Users page loads correctly
Frontend: Lock icon button opens PermissionsDialog
Frontend: PermissionsDialog shows 'Ekran Yetkileri' title
Frontend: agency_admin users see admin notice instead of checkboxes
Frontend: agency_agent users see 10 screen checkboxes
Frontend: 'Tumunu Sec' (Select All) button visible
Frontend: 'Temizle' (Clear) button visible
Frontend: 'Kaydet' (Save) button visible
Frontend: Selecting screens and saving updates permissions successfully
Frontend: 'Yetkiler guncellendi' toast shown after save
Frontend: Badge count shown on Lock button for users with restrictions
Frontend: Navigation filtering implemented in AppShell.jsx (filterNavByMode)
Frontend: Route guard implemented in AppShell.jsx (redirects unauthorized URLs)
critical_code_review_comments: Good: AGENCY_SCREEN_DEFINITIONS properly defined in admin_agency_users.py with 10 screens
Good: _sanitize_auth_user in auth.py includes allowed_screens for all auth flows
Good: AuthUser schema in main.py includes allowed_screens field
Good: AllUsersUserOut includes allowed_screens for admin user list
Good: PermissionsDialog correctly differentiates agency_admin (shows notice) vs agency_agent (shows checkboxes)
Good: filterNavByMode in AppShell.jsx correctly filters nav items by userAllowedScreens
Good: Route guard in AppShell.jsx redirects unauthorized URL access
Good: Invalid screen keys are filtered out server-side in update endpoint
Minor: Consider adding aria-describedby to DialogContent for better accessibility
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing agency_agent users for permission testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 57 verified the Granular User Permissions feature: (1) GET /api/admin/permissions/screens returns 10 screen definitions, (2) GET/PUT /api/admin/all-users/{user_id}/permissions manages user's allowed_screens array, (3) Login and /api/auth/me include allowed_screens in response, (4) Frontend PermissionsDialog accessible via Lock button in All Users table Actions column, (5) agency_admin users see green notice about full access, agency_agent users see checkboxes for each screen, (6) Navigation filtering in AppShell.jsx filters sidebar items based on user.allowed_screens, (7) Route guard redirects unauthorized URL access to /app. Two users have restrictions: testuser1_140038@example.com and expireduser_140131@example.com with [dashboard, rezervasyonlar, oteller].

mocked_apis.notes: All APIs return real data from MongoDB. No mocked endpoints.
